title: The future: write the correct form
lesson: Use 'will' + the base verb for predictions, on-the-spot decisions, offers, and promises. The form is the same for every subject (I will, she will), and the negative is 'will not', contracted to 'won't'. For plans and intentions already decided, use 'be going to' + base verb (I'm going to study).

items.explanation: Future decisions and promises use will + base verb: will call.
Predictions about the future use will + base verb: will rain.
Negative future: will not / won't + base verb.
'be' keeps its base form after will: will be.
Offers and future actions use will + base verb: will help.

items.text: The phone is ringing. I {1} it.
items.explanation: An on-the-spot decision made at the moment of speaking uses will + base verb: will answer.
A promise not to do something uses the negative future: won't / will not + base verb.

items.text: Look at those dark clouds! It {1}.
items.explanation: When there is present evidence for a prediction, we use 'be going to' + base verb: it is going to rain.

items.text: She has bought her ticket. She {1} Spain next week.
items.explanation: A plan already decided uses 'be going to' + base verb: she is going to visit.

items.text: We {1} a new car next year.
items.explanation: An intention or plan uses 'be going to' + base verb: we are going to buy.

items.text: I think our team {1} the match.
items.explanation: An opinion or prediction with 'I think' uses will + base verb: will win.
